Title: Co-opting the fermentation pathway for tombusvirus replication: Compartmentalization of cellular metabolic pathways for rapid ATP generation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides local energy for viral replication processes, including replicase assembly and ATP-dependent host factor function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports tombusvirus replication and local ATP generation in replication compartments
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pdc1 depletion together with loss of Pdc5 caused ~10-fold reduction in TBSV repRNA accumulation in yeast. 2. Enzymatically functional Pdc1, but not the low-activity Pdc1S455F mutant, supported TBSV replication. 3. Pdc1 directly interacts with tombusvirus replication proteins, is recruited into replication compartments, and is required for efficient local ATP production and replicase assembly.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyruvate decarboxylase family; thiamine pyrophosphate-dependent decarboxylase
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast depletion mutant; complementation; catalytic mutant analysis; MYTH assay; co-purification; pull-down assay; in vitro replicase reconstitution; ATP FRET biosensor
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; re-localized to viral replication compartment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TBSV p33; TBSV p92pol; CIRV p36; CIRV p95
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts transiently during early replicase assembly; also supports CIRV and FHV replication in yeast; function linked to fermentation-mediated NAD+ regeneration that sustains glycolysis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with Pdc1 to support efficient tombusvirus replication in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Depletion of Pdc1 in the pdc5Δ background strongly reduced TBSV replication. 2. The double perturbation indicates that Pdc5 contributes overlapping fermentation support for viral replication.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not experimentally characterized in as much depth as Pdc1; implicated through paralog redundancy with Pdc1

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports local ATP generation by regenerating NAD+ for glycolysis in viral replication compartments
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tombusvirus replication and ATP accumulation within replication compartments; knockdown reduces viral RNA accumulation in plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Adh1 directly interacts with tombusvirus replication proteins and is recruited into replication compartments. 2. Adh1 knockdown in N. benthamiana reduced TBSV, CNV, and CIRV accumulation by ~2-fold. 3. Adh1 knockdown lowered ATP levels inside replication compartments, supporting a role in fermentation-driven local energy production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MYTH assay; co-purification; pull-down assay; VIGS knockdown; confocal localization; BiFC; ATP FRET biosensor
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol; re-localized to viral replication compartment
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TBSV p33; TBSV p92pol; CIRV p36; CIRV p95; BaMV ORF1 capping domain; BaMV ORF1 helicase domain
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role is interpreted through NAD+ regeneration for glycolysis rather than direct ATP synthesis; induced by TBSV, CNV, BaMV and TMV infection in plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive upstream regulator required for efficient TBSV replication via controlling PDC1/PDC5 expression
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Depletion of Pdc2 reduced TBSV repRNA accumulation by ~3-fold in yeast. 2. The result supports the importance of the fermentation pathway in viral replication.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a regulator, it is experimentally used only as supporting evidence for the role of fermentation genes and is not a main focal metabolism regulator in the paper
